compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Additional failure mode (same review-plan subsystem): during this run, review-plan's attested-mode git handling (git fetch/stash to compute the signature at HEAD) FAILED mid-operation (the 'cannot lock ref refs/remotes/origin/tickets' race under 4 parallel reviews) and left a stray stash APPLIED into the working tree, injecting <<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>> merge-conflict markers into 4 unrelated core files (src/rebar/config.py, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py, infra/gerrit/materialize-g2p-config.sh). This BROKE the working tree (rebar failed to import; all tests errored at collection) until manually resolved via git checkout HEAD. review-plan must (a) never run concurrently in the same worktree without a lock, and (b) guarantee working-tree restoration is atomic / rolled back on failure — a fail-closed abort must not leave a half-applied stash. AC candidate: review-plan git handling is crash-safe (no working-tree mutation survives a failed run).

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

Re-running `rebar review-plan` on the **already-signed, unchanged** auto-lander epic
`f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af` (and its story S1 `843b-801f-9ef9-442d`) produced a stream of
**invalid blocking findings** and **non-convergent** behaviour: the plan had been signed
CLEAN (`blocking: 0, advisory: 2`) at HEAD `2272d68`, and re-review at a later HEAD
`6244666` — with **no change to the plan**, only `main` advancing — returned `BLOCK`
(`blocking: 1, advisory: 13`). Remediating the findings then *increased* the finding count
and *spawned new blocks*. The gate never converged; the epic could only be claimed via
`--force`.

This bug documents each invalid/problematic finding with full context, its triggering
criterion, its round-over-round progression, and — crucially — **which stage of the review
pipeline (finding discovery / validation assessment / impact assessment) should have caught
it and why.** It is the raw material for calibrating the plan-review gate so an already-signed
plan is not re-blocked by non-deterministic false positives at a drifted HEAD.

## The review pipeline (stage vocabulary used below)

`review-plan` produces a verdict through three conceptual stages. Each invalid finding below
is attributed to the stage that should have filtered it:

1. **Finding discovery** — generation of candidate findings from the plan + graph. A finding
   whose premise is *contradicted by text present in the plan* should never be emitted here.
2. **Validation assessment** — deciding whether a candidate is *real*. A candidate that is
   contradicted by (a) the plan itself, (b) a sibling finding in the same verdict, or (c) the
   ticket's own recorded comment trail should be dropped as not-real here.
3. **Impact assessment** — scoring severity and deriving block-vs-advisory. A finding whose
   remedy is deferred/operator-attested/implementation-stage should not score at the blocking
   tier here.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Take a plan that has a valid, signed `plan-review` attestation (`blocking: 0`).
2. Let `main` advance so the attestation reads *stale* (HEAD-relative staleness) without
   editing the ticket.
3. Run `rebar review-plan <id>` again. Observe a materially different verdict — new blocking
   findings that were not present in the signing review, including at least one whose fix is
   already in the plan text.
4. Remediate a flagged advisory (e.g. "name the missing edit target in Scope"). Re-run the
   review. Observe that the remediation *introduces new blocking findings* (it now demands an
   AC + proving command for the thing you just added to Scope).

**Expected:** re-reviewing an unchanged, previously-signed plan at a drifted HEAD yields a
substantively stable verdict (still a PASS, or at most the same findings). Findings whose
premise is present-and-contradicted in the plan are dropped in validation. Remediating an
advisory does not manufacture new blocks.

**Actual:** the verdict flips PASS→BLOCK with no plan change; a blocking finding re-appears
verbatim after its fix is demonstrably present; two findings in the same verdict contradict
each other; and advisory remediation cascades into new blocks. The gate does not converge.

---

## A. Invalid BLOCKING findings

### A1 — Epic: "pre-cutover snapshot never captured / not stored" (FALSE POSITIVE)
- **Criteria:** `E2`, `F1`. **Impact:** 0.85 (blocking). **Rounds:** raised r1 → remediated
  (AC added naming actor, artifact, location, timing) → **re-raised VERBATIM r2**.
- **Finding text:** *"The security-invariants AC requires 'a GET /projects/rebar/access diff
  vs the pre-cutover snapshot,' but the plan never tasks anyone with capturing the pre-cutover
  snapshot before the label is deployed, nor where it is stored."*
- **Why invalid:** after r1 the plan literally contained: *"Pre-cutover access baseline
  (captured by S1 843b, BEFORE the label is pushed): save the current `GET
  /a/projects/rebar/access` response to `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json`
  and commit it in the same change."* This names the **actor** (S1), the **artifact/location**
  (the JSON file), and the **timing** (before cutover, same change) — exactly the three things
  the finding claims are absent. The finding offered **no `checklist_item` fix** (it had
  nothing to suggest because the fix was already present).
- **Stage that should have caught it:** **Validation assessment.** A validation pass that
  re-reads the plan for the finding's own premise would find the contradicting text and drop
  the candidate. Secondarily **finding discovery** should not regenerate a finding whose
  subject string is present in the plan.
- **Damning corroboration:** the SAME r2 verdict contains advisory A-adv "the parent
  **explicitly assigns capture to S1**" (see B2 below). One verdict simultaneously asserts
  "no one is tasked with capture" (blocking) and "the parent explicitly assigns capture to S1"
  (advisory). A validation stage that cross-checked findings against each other would have
  killed one of them.

### A2 — S1: "setup-project.sh has no AC / no proving command" and "line-148 commit message not updated" (REMEDIATION-INDUCED)
- **Criteria:** `E6`/`F1` and `G1G2`. **Impact:** 0.85 each (blocking). **Rounds:** absent r1 →
  **created by remediating epic advisory A-#6** ("add `setup-project.sh` to the plan's edit
  targets"). I added a Scope sentence naming `setup-project.sh`; r2 then blocked because that
  Scope deliverable had no matching AC/proving command.
- **Why problematic:** this is the **remediation cascade** — acting on a low-impact advisory
  manufactured two *blocking* findings. A gate that turns "you should also mention X" into "X
  is now a blocking deliverable with no AC" cannot converge: every remediation widens the
  surface faster than it closes it. (These two were legitimately fixable and I fixed them with
  one AC + `grep -F 'Autosubmit' infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`; the DEFECT is that the gate
  created them from an advisory in the first place.)
- **Stage that should have caught it:** **Impact assessment.** A newly-named, secondary
  audit-trail touch (a commit-message string) is not a blocking-tier gap; it is advisory at
  most. Escalating it to `blocking` (0.85) is impact inflation.

---

## B. Invalid / non-material ADVISORY findings

### B1 — Epic A8: "sibling tickets touch the same file with no ordering edge"
- **Criteria:** `P5`. **Impact:** 0.4. **Fix offered:** none.
- **Why invalid:** `infra/autolander/loop.py` is co-edited by S2a/S2b/S2c **by deliberate
  decomposition** (S2 was split into three tasks precisely to keep each unit loadable — the
  module-size ethos), and real ordering edges DO exist (`38be depends_on 89f9` and `08e1`).
  The premise ("no ordering edge") is false, and the pattern it flags is the intended design.
- **Stage:** **Validation assessment** — a dependency-graph read disproves "no ordering edge."

### B2 — Epic A9 / S1 A7: "Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues"
- **Criteria:** `P6`. **Impact:** 0.4. **Fix offered:** none. **Rounds:** present both rounds.
- **Why invalid:** contentless. No specific AC is named, no defect is described, no remediation
  is suggested. It is un-actionable by construction — the agent cannot act on "quality issues."
- **Stage:** **Finding discovery** should not emit a finding with an empty body and no
  `checklist_item`; a finding with no actionable content is noise, not signal.

### B3 — Epic A12: "`POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` endpoint unverified"
- **Criteria:** `T3`. **Impact:** 0.0. **Fix offered:** add a live API probe.
- **Why invalid:** this exact claim was **already litigated and rejected in the ticket's own
  comment trail** — the epic records: *"Verified against the Gerrit REST API docs: `POST
  /changes/{change-id}/rebase:chain` DOES exist (3.6+, so the 3.14.1 instance has it) — the
  review's own advisory T3 concedes this."* The gate re-surfaced a settled, fact-checked
  point that a prior review round had already conceded.
- **Stage:** **Validation assessment** — the ticket comment trail is in scope for the review
  and directly refutes the finding. Re-raising conceded fact is a validation miss.

### B4 — Epic A11 & A13: "`rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` trailer-preservation unverified"
- **Criteria:** `T1`, `T3`. **Impact:** 0.0 (both). **Fix offered:** add a probe/smoke test.
- **Why non-material:** these ask for *implementation-stage verification* of a documented
  Gerrit flag. The plan already routes that verification into child ACs — S2a carries an
  "uploader/trailer-preservation test" and S5b carries a "synthetic scratch-ref smoke change."
  The plan does not need to re-prove a library capability at plan-review time.
- **Stage:** **Impact assessment** correctly scored these 0.0 (non-blocking), but they were
  still surfaced as duplicated findings (A11 and A13 are the same claim). **Finding discovery**
  should de-duplicate; a 0.0-impact, already-covered concern is noise.

### B5 — Epic A14: "S5 should formally `depends_on` S3, not merely 'Uses S3'"
- **Criteria:** `G4`. **Impact:** 0.0.
- **Why non-material:** S5 already documents "Uses S3's outcome marker," S5 is gated behind S2,
  and S3 completes in the same topological tier. The distinction between a `Uses` note and a
  formal `depends_on` here is cosmetic and impact-0.0 by the reviewer's own scoring.
- **Stage:** **Impact assessment** (correctly 0.0) — but flagging it at all, given it is
  cosmetic, is discovery noise.

### B6 — S1 A4/A5/A6/A8/A9/A11: "pin curl/auth/jq/rollback into the plan ACs"
- **Criteria:** `T3`, `E6`, `T5b`, `T5c`. **Impact:** 0.85 down to 0.0.
- **Why non-material:** these demand that the plan AC embed the exact `curl` command, auth
  mechanism, `jq` assertion, HTTP-200 check, and rollback procedure for the **operator cutover**
  (`GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/...`, `GET /a/projects/rebar/access`). That cutover is an
  **operator-attested** step (ADR-0043) whose evidence is a recorded attestation, not an
  in-session command. Mechanical proving-commands belong in the cutover script/runbook, not the
  plan AC.
- **Stage:** **Impact assessment.** The gate does not distinguish codebase-verifiable ACs from
  operator-attested ones (ADR-0043); it applies the "every AC needs an in-session proving
  command" rule uniformly, inflating operator-runbook granularity to 0.85 blocking-adjacent.

### B7 — S1 A12: "no RETIRE path for the Autosubmit label"
- **Criteria:** `T9`. **Impact:** 0.0.
- **Why invalid:** demanding a feature-retirement procedure for a label being *introduced* is
  YAGNI/scope creep. The label ships with a documented back-out note already; a full retire
  runbook is out of scope for the introduction story.
- **Stage:** **Validation assessment** (scope check) should recognise retire-path demands on an
  introduction ticket as out-of-scope, not a plan gap.

---

## C. Cross-cutting review-gate defects (the root causes)

1. **Non-determinism across HEAD drift (the core bug).** The epic was signed CLEAN
   (`blocking: 0`) at `2272d68`; the identical plan re-reviewed at `6244666` returned
   `blocking: 1, advisory: 13`. No plan change occurred — only `main` advanced. Re-review of an
   unchanged plan must be substantively stable; it is not. This is what forces the entire
   re-claim treadmill.
2. **Verbatim re-block after a demonstrated fix (A1).** Validation does not re-read the plan for
   a finding's own premise before re-emitting it.
3. **Intra-verdict self-contradiction (A1 blocking vs its advisory twin).** Findings within one
   verdict are not cross-checked for consistency.
4. **Remediation-induced finding cascade (A2).** Acting on a low-impact advisory manufactures
   new blocking findings, so the finding count rises with each remediation round and the gate
   never converges.
5. **Uniform proving-command demand ignores ADR-0043 (B6).** Operator-attested ACs are scored
   as if they need in-session mechanical commands.
6. **Contentless and duplicated findings (B2, B4).** Findings with empty bodies / no
   `checklist_item`, and duplicate findings, pass discovery.
7. **Hard-override mis-classification is the blocking amplifier (root cause of the 0.85s).**
   Per the impact model shipped in closed story `0858-facb-6901-4781` (under closed epic
   `4cc3-23c9-b062-41ac`), a finding tagged with any of {AC-unverifiable, DoD-uncertifiable,
   undecomposed, divergent-implementation} gets `impact_sev` **force-set to ≥ 0.85** (a hard
   override, bypassing the severity MAX). Nearly every invalid finding above lands at exactly
   **0.85** — i.e. the validation stage mis-tagged a false-positive/non-material finding into
   a hard-override axis (chiefly "AC-unverifiable"), which then mechanically escalated it to
   the blocking tier. The override is doing its job; the DEFECT is upstream — validation
   assigns the hard-override axis to findings that are not actually AC-unverifiable (the AC is
   present, or the "unverifiable" step is operator-attested per ADR-0043). **Fixing validation
   mis-tagging is higher-leverage than re-tuning the threshold**, because the override
   deliberately ignores the threshold.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Finding discovery drops (or does not emit) any candidate finding whose subject text is
      **present and contradicted** in the plan body; a regression fixture reproduces epic A1
      (snapshot AC present) and asserts no such block is emitted.
- [ ] Validation assessment cross-checks findings **within a single verdict** for mutual
      contradiction and drops the weaker/contradicted one; a fixture reproduces the A1
      blocking-vs-advisory contradiction and asserts at most one survives.
- [ ] Validation assessment consults the ticket's **recorded comment trail** and drops a
      finding that re-litigates a point the trail already resolved; a fixture reproduces B3
      (`rebase:chain` conceded in-trail) and asserts it is dropped.
- [ ] Impact assessment distinguishes **operator-attested** criteria (ADR-0043) from
      codebase-verifiable ones and does NOT demand an in-session proving command for the former;
      a fixture reproduces B6 and asserts those findings are not scored at the blocking tier.
- [ ] Finding discovery **de-duplicates** findings and suppresses contentless findings (empty
      body / no `checklist_item`); a fixture reproduces B2/B4.
- [ ] **Re-review stability (the headline criterion):** re-running `review-plan` on an
      unchanged, previously-signed plan at a drifted HEAD yields a verdict with **no NEW
      blocking findings** relative to the signing review; a fixture (sign at HEAD-A, advance to
      HEAD-B with no ticket edit, re-review) asserts the block set does not grow.
- [ ] A short design note (or ADR) records the "remediation cascade" anti-pattern (acting on an
      advisory must not manufacture a block) and the re-review-stability contract.

## Notes / provenance

Surfaced while executing the auto-lander epic `f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af` under `/rebar-implement`:
every one of its 13 live tickets was signed at `2272d68` and went stale when `main` advanced,
so each claim required a fresh review, exposing the non-convergence. Full per-finding triage
and the force-claim justifications are recorded on session log `547b-4aca-0076-4a36`.

**Related prior work (both CLOSED):** epic `4cc3-23c9-b062-41ac` "Review-gate impact redesign
+ threshold re-tune (plan-review + code-review)" and its story `0858-facb-6901-4781`
"Plan-review impact redesign: severity-first MAX + hard override + detection amplifier" — this
bug is a follow-on defect against that same subsystem (see cross-cutting defect #7: the
hard-override from `0858` is what force-escalates the mis-validated findings to 0.85). Linked
`relates_to 4cc3` and `discovered_from f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af`.
compiled_state.title: Plan-review gate: non-convergent re-review of an already-signed plan at drifted HEAD emits invalid blocking findings (auto-lander epic f1fa)
